steps.title: Identify the structural pattern
steps.content: The passage opens with a generalization: “I have always noticed that it is the fixed idea of each member of the crew that he is doing everything.” The remainder provides specific examples—Harris's view and George's view—from the boat situation.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
Choice D describes the exact structure: a generalization supported by specific examples from the situation.

Choices A, B, and C each miss the mark: A misreads the tone (unexpected vs. habitual), B misfocuses (narrator's role vs. crew dynamics), and C mischaracterizes the passage (contributions vs. beliefs).

This matches choice D—D is correct.
